import os
import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.contrib.syndication.views import Feed
from django.test import RequestFactory

settings.configure(
    ROOT_URLCONF=__name__,
    ALLOWED_HOSTS=['*'],
)
django.setup()

class MyFeed(Feed):
    title = "My Feed"
    link = "/feed/"
    description = "My Feed Description"

    def items(self):
        return [1]

    def item_title(self, item):
        return f"Item {item}"

    def item_link(self, item):
        return f"/item/{item}/"

    def item_comments(self, item):
        return f"https://example.com/item/{item}/comments"

request = RequestFactory().get('/feed/')
feed = MyFeed()
feedgen = feed.get_feed(None, request)

# Check if comments are in the feed items
for item in feedgen.items:
    print(f"Item comments: {item.get('comments')}")
    if item.get('comments') == "https://example.com/item/1/comments":
        print("SUCCESS: comments found in feed item")
    else:
        print("FAILURE: comments not found in feed item")
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > patch_field.py
import os

file_path = 'django/db/models/fields/__init__.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_eq = """    def __eq__(self, other):
        # Needed for @total_ordering
        if isinstance(other, Field):
            return self.creation_counter == other.creation_counter
        return NotImplemented"""

new_eq = """    def __eq__(self, other):
        # Needed for @total_ordering
        if isinstance(other, Field):
            return (
                self.creation_counter == other.creation_counter and
                getattr(self, 'model', None) == getattr(other, 'model', None)
            )
        return NotImplemented"""

old_lt = """    def __lt__(self, other):
        # This is needed because bisect does not take a comparison function.
        if isinstance(other, Field):
            return self.creation_counter < other.creation_counter
        return NotImplemented"""

new_lt = """    def __lt__(self, other):
        # This is needed because bisect does not take a comparison function.
        if isinstance(other, Field):
            if self.creation_counter != other.creation_counter:
                return self.creation_counter < other.creation_counter
            elif getattr(self, 'model', None) != getattr(other, 'model', None):
                return str(getattr(self, 'model', None)) < str(getattr(other, 'model', None))
            return False
        return NotImplemented"""

old_hash = """    def __hash__(self):
        return hash(self.creation_counter)"""

new_hash = """    def __hash__(self):
        return hash((self.creation_counter, getattr(self, 'model', None)))"""

content = content.replace(old_eq, new_eq)
content = content.replace(old_lt, new_lt)
content = content.replace(old_hash, new_hash)

with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF
python3 patch_field.py

cat << 'EOF' > patch.py
import os

file_path = 'django/db/models/sql/query.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """        elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
            # The items of the iterable may be expressions and therefore need
            # to be resolved independently.
            return type(value)(
                self.resolve_lookup_value(sub_value, can_reuse, allow_joins)
                for sub_value in value
            )"""

new_code = """        elif isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
            # The items of the iterable may be expressions and therefore need
            # to be resolved independently.
            resolved_values = (
                self.resolve_lookup_value(sub_value, can_reuse, allow_joins)
                for sub_value in value
            )
            if hasattr(value, '_make'):
                return type(value)._make(resolved_values)
            return type(value)(resolved_values)"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find the code to patch.")
EOF

with open('django/db/models/base.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old = """        if (
            cls._meta.pk.auto_created and
            not settings.is_overridden('DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD') and
            not cls._meta.app_config._is_default_auto_field_overridden
        ):"""

new = """        if (
            cls._meta.pk.auto_created and
            not cls._meta.parents and
            not settings.is_overridden('DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD') and
            not cls._meta.app_config._is_default_auto_field_overridden
        ):"""

if old in content:
    content = content.replace(old, new)
    with open('django/db/models/base.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:

import re

with open('django/db/models/expressions.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

rand_replacement = """    def __rand__(self, other):
        if getattr(self, 'conditional', False) and getattr(other, 'conditional', False):
            return Q(other) & Q(self)
        raise NotImplementedError(
            "Use .bitand() and .bitor() for bitwise logical operations."
        )"""

ror_replacement = """    def __ror__(self, other):
        if getattr(self, 'conditional', False) and getattr(other, 'conditional', False):
            return Q(other) | Q(self)
        raise NotImplementedError(
            "Use .bitand() and .bitor() for bitwise logical operations."
        )"""

content = re.sub(r'    def __rand__\(self, other\):\n        raise NotImplementedError\(\n            "Use \.bitand\(\) and \.bitor\(\) for bitwise logical operations\."\n        \)', rand_replacement, content)
content = re.sub(r'    def __ror__\(self, other\):\n        raise NotImplementedError\(\n            "Use \.bitand\(\) and \.bitor\(\) for bitwise logical operations\."\n        \)', ror_replacement, content)

with open('django/db/models/expressions.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.urls import path
from django.http import HttpResponse
import functools

def my_view(arg1, request, arg2):
    return HttpResponse(f"arg1={arg1}, arg2={arg2}")

p = functools.partial(my_view, "partial_arg")

urlpatterns = [
    path('test/<str:arg2>/', p),
]

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    ROOT_URLCONF=__name__,
    SECRET_KEY='secret',
    ALLOWED_HOSTS=['*'],
    MIDDLEWARE=[],
)
django.setup()

# Monkeypatch ResolverMatch.__init__ to unwrap partial
from django.urls.resolvers import ResolverMatch
orig_init = ResolverMatch.__init__
def new_init(self, func, args, kwargs, *a, **kw):
    if isinstance(func, functools.partial):
        args = func.args + args
        kwargs = {**func.keywords, **kwargs}
        func = func.func
    orig_init(self, func, args, kwargs, *a, **kw)
ResolverMatch.__init__ = new_init

from django.test import Client
c = Client()
try:
    response = c.get('/test/url_arg/')
    print(response.content.decode())
except Exception as e:
    print("Exception:", e)

    path('test/<str:arg2>/', p),
]

settings.configure(
    DEBUG=True,
    ROOT_URLCONF=__name__,
    SECRET_KEY='secret',
    ALLOWED_HOSTS=['*'],
    MIDDLEWARE=[],
)
django.setup()

from django.test import Client
c = Client()
response = c.get('/test/url_arg/')
print("Wrapped:", response.content.decode())

# Monkeypatch ResolverMatch.__init__ to unwrap partial
from django.urls.resolvers import ResolverMatch
orig_init = ResolverMatch.__init__
def new_init(self, func, args, kwargs, *a, **kw):
    if isinstance(func, functools.partial):
        args = func.args + args
        kwargs = {**func.keywords, **kwargs}
        func = func.func
    orig_init(self, func, args, kwargs, *a, **kw)
ResolverMatch.__init__ = new_init

response = c.get('/test/url_arg/')
print("Unwrapped:", response.content.decode())
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > patch_ndarithmetic.py
import sys

file_path = "astropy/nddata/mixins/ndarithmetic.py"
with open(file_path, "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

start_idx = -1
end_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "# If only one mask is present we need not bother about any type checks" in line:
        start_idx = i + 1
    if "def _arithmetic_wcs" in line:
        end_idx = i - 1
        break

if start_idx != -1 and end_idx != -1:
    new_lines = lines[:start_idx] + [
        "        if handle_mask is None:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        elif self.mask is None and (operand is None or operand.mask is None):\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        elif self.mask is None and operand is not None:\n",
        "            # Make a copy so there is no reference in the result.\n",
        "            return deepcopy(operand.mask)\n",
        "        elif operand is None or operand.mask is None:\n",
        "            return deepcopy(self.mask)\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            # Now lets calculate the resulting mask (operation enforces copy)\n",
        "            return handle_mask(self.mask, operand.mask, **kwds)\n",
        "\n"
    ] + lines[end_idx:]
    
    with open(file_path, "w") as f:
        f.writelines(new_lines)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find the block to patch.")
EOF

cat << 'EOF' >> astropy/nddata/mixins/tests/test_ndarithmetic.py

def test_arithmetic_mask_with_none_operand_mask():
    # Test for issue where mask propagation fails when one operand does not have a mask
    # and handle_mask is not None (e.g., np.bitwise_or)
    array = np.array([[0, 1, 0], [1, 0, 1], [0, 1, 0]])
    mask = np.array([[0, 1, 64], [8, 0, 1], [2, 1, 0]])

    nref_nomask = NDDataRef(array)
    nref_mask = NDDataRef(array, mask=mask)

    # multiply mask by constant (mask * no mask)
    res1 = nref_mask.multiply(1., handle_mask=np.bitwise_or)
    assert np.array_equal(res1.mask, mask)

    # multiply mask by no mask (mask * no mask)
    res2 = nref_mask.multiply(nref_nomask, handle_mask=np.bitwise_or)
    assert np.array_equal(res2.mask, mask)

    # multiply no mask by mask (no mask * mask)
    res3 = nref_nomask.multiply(nref_mask, handle_mask=np.bitwise_or)
    assert np.array_equal(res3.mask, mask)
EOF

import sys

file_path = "astropy/nddata/mixins/ndarithmetic.py"
with open(file_path, "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

start_idx = -1
end_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "def _arithmetic_data(self, operation, operand, **kwds):" in line:
        start_idx = i
    if "def _arithmetic_uncertainty(" in line:
        end_idx = i - 1
        break

if start_idx != -1 and end_idx != -1:
    new_lines = lines[:start_idx] + [
        "    def _arithmetic_data(self, operation, operand, **kwds):\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        Calculate the resulting data.\n",
        "\n",
        "        Parameters\n",
        "        ----------\n",
        "        operation : callable\n",
        "            see `NDArithmeticMixin._arithmetic` parameter description.\n",
        "\n",
        "        operand : `NDData`-like instance\n",
        "            The second operand wrapped in an instance of the same class as\n",
        "            self.\n",
        "\n",
        "        kwds :\n",
        "            Additional parameters.\n",
        "\n",
        "        Returns\n",
        "        -------\n",
        "        result_data : ndarray or `~astropy.units.Quantity`\n",
        "            If both operands had no unit the resulting data is a simple numpy\n",
        "            array, but if any of the operands had a unit the return is a\n",
        "            Quantity.\n",
        "        \"\"\"\n",
        "        # Do the calculation with or without units\n",
        "        if operand is None:\n",
        "            if self.unit is None:\n",
        "                result = operation(self.data, **kwds)\n",
        "            else:\n",
        "                result = operation(self.data << self.unit, **kwds)\n",
        "        elif self.unit is None:\n",
        "            if operand.unit is None:\n",
        "                result = operation(self.data, operand.data, **kwds)\n",
        "            else:\n",
        "                result = operation(\n",
        "                    self.data << dimensionless_unscaled, operand.data << operand.unit, **kwds\n",
        "                )\n",
        "        elif hasattr(operand, \"unit\"):\n",
        "            if operand.unit is not None:\n",
        "                result = operation(self.data << self.unit, operand.data << operand.unit, **kwds)\n",
        "            else:\n",
        "                result = operation(\n",
        "                    self.data << self.unit, operand.data << dimensionless_unscaled, **kwds\n",
        "                )\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            result = operation(self.data << self.unit, operand.data << operand.unit, **kwds)\n",
        "\n",
        "        return result\n",
        "\n"
    ] + lines[end_idx:]
    
    with open(file_path, "w") as f:
        f.writelines(new_lines)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find the block to patch.")
EOF

import sys

file_path = "astropy/nddata/mixins/ndarithmetic.py"
with open(file_path, "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Patch _arithmetic_mask
start_idx = -1
end_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "# If only one mask is present we need not bother about any type checks" in line:
        start_idx = i + 1
    if "def _arithmetic_wcs" in line:
        end_idx = i - 1
        break

if start_idx != -1 and end_idx != -1:
    lines = lines[:start_idx] + [
        "        if handle_mask is None:\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        elif self.mask is None and (operand is None or operand.mask is None):\n",
        "            return None\n",
        "        elif self.mask is None and operand is not None:\n",
        "            # Make a copy so there is no reference in the result.\n",
        "            return deepcopy(operand.mask)\n",
        "        elif operand is None or operand.mask is None:\n",
        "            return deepcopy(self.mask)\n",
        "        else:\n",
        "            # Now lets calculate the resulting mask (operation enforces copy)\n",
        "            return handle_mask(self.mask, operand.mask, **kwds)\n",
        "\n"
    ] + lines[end_idx:]

# Patch _arithmetic_meta
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "if not self.meta:" in line and "kwargs[\"meta\"] = deepcopy(operand.meta)" in lines[i+1]:
        lines[i] = "            if not self.meta and hasattr(operand, \"meta\"):\n"
        break

# Patch _arithmetic_wcs and _arithmetic_meta
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "if not compare_wcs(self.wcs, operand.wcs, **kwds):" in line:
        lines[i] = "        if operand is not None and not compare_wcs(self.wcs, operand.wcs, **kwds):\n"
    if "return handle_meta(self.meta, operand.meta, **kwds)" in line:
        lines[i] = "        return handle_meta(self.meta, operand.meta if operand is not None else None, **kwds)\n"

with open(file_path, "w") as f:
    f.writelines(lines)
print("Patched successfully.")

cat << 'EOF' > django/contrib/auth/migrations/0011_update_proxy_permissions.py
from django.db import migrations
from django.db.models import Q


def update_proxy_model_permissions(apps, schema_editor, reverse=False):
    """
    Update the content_type of proxy model permissions to use the ContentType
    of the proxy model.
    """
    Permission = apps.get_model('auth', 'Permission')
    ContentType = apps.get_model('contenttypes', 'ContentType')
    for Model in apps.get_models():
        opts = Model._meta
        if not opts.proxy:
            continue
        proxy_default_permissions_codenames = [
            '%s_%s' % (action, opts.model_name)
            for action in opts.default_permissions
        ]
        permissions_query = Q(codename__in=proxy_default_permissions_codenames)
        for codename, name in opts.permissions:
            permissions_query = permissions_query | Q(codename=codename, name=name)
        concrete_content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(Model, for_concrete_model=True)
        proxy_content_type = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(Model, for_concrete_model=False)
        old_content_type = proxy_content_type if reverse else concrete_content_type
        new_content_type = concrete_content_type if reverse else proxy_content_type
        
        old_permissions = Permission.objects.filter(
            permissions_query,
            content_type=old_content_type,
        )
        for old_permission in old_permissions:
            try:
                new_permission = Permission.objects.get(
                    content_type=new_content_type,
                    codename=old_permission.codename,
                )
            except Permission.DoesNotExist:
                old_permission.content_type = new_content_type
                old_permission.save(update_fields=['content_type'])
            else:
                if hasattr(new_permission, 'user_set') and hasattr(old_permission, 'user_set'):
                    new_permission.user_set.add(*old_permission.user_set.all())
                if hasattr(new_permission, 'group_set') and hasattr(old_permission, 'group_set'):
                    new_permission.group_set.add(*old_permission.group_set.all())
                old_permission.delete()


def revert_proxy_model_permissions(apps, schema_editor):
    """
    Update the content_type of proxy model permissions to use the ContentType
    of the concrete model.
    """
    update_proxy_model_permissions(apps, schema_editor, reverse=True)


class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    dependencies = [
        ('auth', '0010_alter_group_name_max_length'),
        ('contenttypes', '0002_remove_content_type_name'),
    ]
    operations = [
        migrations.RunPython(update_proxy_model_permissions, revert_proxy_model_permissions),
    ]
EOF

import sys

file_path = 'django/db/migrations/serializer.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """class EnumSerializer(BaseSerializer):
    def serialize(self):
        enum_class = self.value.__class__
        module = enum_class.__module__
        return "%s.%s[%r]" % (module, enum_class.__name__, self.value.name), {'import %s' % module}"""

new_code = """class EnumSerializer(BaseSerializer):
    def serialize(self):
        enum_class = self.value.__class__
        module = enum_class.__module__
        if self.value.name in enum_class.__members__:
            return "%s.%s[%r]" % (module, enum_class.__name__, self.value.name), {'import %s' % module}
        v_string, v_imports = serializer_factory(self.value.value).serialize()
        imports = {'import %s' % module, *v_imports}
        return "%s.%s(%s)" % (module, enum_class.__name__, v_string), imports"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find old code.")
EOF

from django.db import migrations, models
import django.db.models.deletion

def create_data(apps, schema_editor):
    ModelB = apps.get_model('testapp', 'ModelB')
    ModelA = apps.get_model('testapp', 'ModelA')
    b = ModelB.objects.create()
    ModelA.objects.create(b=b)

class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    initial = True
    dependencies = []
    operations = [
        migrations.CreateModel(
            name='ModelB',
            fields=[
                ('id', models.AutoField(auto_created=True, primary_key=True, serialize=False, verbose_name='ID')),
            ],
        ),
        migrations.CreateModel(
            name='ModelA',
            fields=[
                ('id', models.AutoField(auto_created=True, primary_key=True, serialize=False, verbose_name='ID')),
                ('b', models.ForeignKey(on_delete=django.db.models.deletion.CASCADE, to='testapp.ModelB')),
            ],
        ),
        migrations.RunPython(create_data),
    ]
""")

django.setup()

class MyTestCase(TransactionTestCase):
    serialized_rollback = True

    def test_something(self):

        self.assertEqual(ModelA.objects.count(), 1)
        self.assertEqual(ModelB.objects.count(), 1)

if __name__ == '__main__':

    runner = DiscoverRunner(verbosity=2)

                ('b', models.ForeignKey(on_delete=django.db.models.deletion.CASCADE, to='testapp.ModelB')),
            ],
        ),
        migrations.RunPython(create_data),

import re

with open('django/db/backends/base/creation.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """    def deserialize_db_from_string(self, data):
        \"\"\"
        Reload the database with data from a string generated by
        the serialize_db_to_string() method.
        \"\"\"
        data = StringIO(data)
        with transaction.atomic(using=self.connection.alias):
            for obj in serializers.deserialize("json", data, using=self.connection.alias):
                obj.save()"""

new_code = """    def deserialize_db_from_string(self, data):
        \"\"\"
        Reload the database with data from a string generated by
        the serialize_db_to_string() method.
        \"\"\"
        data = StringIO(data)
        for obj in serializers.deserialize("json", data, using=self.connection.alias):
            obj.save()"""

content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)

with open('django/db/backends/base/creation.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

import os

file_path = 'django/contrib/staticfiles/handlers.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Add import for sync_to_async
import_str = "from asgiref.sync import sync_to_async\n"
if import_str not in content:
    # Find the first import and insert it there
    lines = content.split('\n')
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if line.startswith('from ') or line.startswith('import '):
            lines.insert(i, import_str.strip())
            break
    content = '\n'.join(lines)

# Add get_response_async
get_response_async_str = """
    async def get_response_async(self, request):
        try:
            return await sync_to_async(self.serve, thread_sensitive=True)(request)
        except Http404 as e:
            return await sync_to_async(response_for_exception, thread_sensitive=True)(request, e)
"""

# Find get_response and insert get_response_async after it
if 'async def get_response_async' not in content:
    content = content.replace(
        "    def get_response(self, request):\n        try:\n            return self.serve(request)\n        except Http404 as e:\n            return response_for_exception(request, e)",
        "    def get_response(self, request):\n        try:\n            return self.serve(request)\n        except Http404 as e:\n            return response_for_exception(request, e)\n" + get_response_async_str
    )

with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

from functools import partial

        }
    },

)

django.setup()

class OneModel(models.Model):
    class Meta:
        ordering = ("-id",)
    id = models.BigAutoField(primary_key=True)
    root = models.ForeignKey("OneModel", on_delete=models.CASCADE, null=True)
    oneval = models.BigIntegerField(null=True)

qs = TwoModel.objects.all()

opts = qs.query.get_meta()

def check_condition(name):
    pieces = name.split('__')
    field, targets, alias, joins, path, opts_out, transform_function = compiler._setup_joins(pieces, opts, None)
    
    cond = (
        field.is_relation and
        opts_out.ordering and
        not isinstance(transform_function, partial) and
        getattr(field, 'attname', None) != pieces[-1] and
        pieces[-1] != 'pk'
    )
    print(f"{name}: {cond}")

check_condition('record__root')
check_condition('record__root_id')
check_condition('record__pk')
check_condition('record__root_id__isnull')

import sys

with open('django/db/migrations/autodetector.py', 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

start_idx = -1
end_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if 'if order_with_respect_to:' in line and 'self.add_operation(' in lines[i+1]:
        start_idx = i
    if start_idx != -1 and i > start_idx and ']' in line and ')' in lines[i+1]:
        end_idx = i + 1
        break

if start_idx != -1 and end_idx != -1:
    order_wrt_lines = lines[start_idx:end_idx+1]
    del lines[start_idx:end_idx+1]
    
    insert_idx = -1
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if 'for index in indexes:' in line and 'self.add_operation(' in lines[i+1]:
            insert_idx = i
            break
            
    if insert_idx != -1:
        lines = lines[:insert_idx] + order_wrt_lines + lines[insert_idx:]
        with open('django/db/migrations/autodetector.py', 'w') as f:
            f.writelines(lines)
        print("Patched successfully.")
    else:
        print("Could not find insert index.")
else:
    print("Could not find order_with_respect_to block.")
EOF

    INSTALLED_APPS=['__main__'],
)
django.setup()

class Category(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class Item(models.Model):
    category = models.ForeignKey(Category, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)

class Order(models.Model):
    # limit_choices_to with a join
    category = models.ForeignKey(
        Category, 
        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
        limit_choices_to=models.Q(item__name__icontains='apple')
    )

class OrderForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Order
        fields = ['category']

for choice in form.fields['category'].choices:
    print(choice)

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import models
from django.forms import ModelForm

settings.configure(
    DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}},
    INSTALLED_APPS=['__main__'],
)
django.setup()

class Category(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta:
        app_label = '__main__'

class Item(models.Model):
    category = models.ForeignKey(Category, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    class Meta:
        app_label = '__main__'

class Order(models.Model):
    # limit_choices_to with a join
    category = models.ForeignKey(
        Category, 
        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
        limit_choices_to=models.Q(item__name__icontains='apple')
    )
    class Meta:
        app_label = '__main__'

class OrderForm(ModelForm):
    class Meta:
        model = Order
        fields = ['category']

from django.db import connection
with connection.schema_editor() as schema_editor:
    schema_editor.create_model(Category)
    schema_editor.create_model(Item)
    schema_editor.create_model(Order)

c1 = Category.objects.create(name='Fruit')
Item.objects.create(category=c1, name='Red Apple')
Item.objects.create(category=c1, name='Green Apple')

def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):
    """Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed."""
    if hasattr(formfield, 'queryset') and hasattr(formfield, 'get_limit_choices_to'):
        limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()
        if limit_choices_to is not None:
            if hasattr(formfield.queryset, 'query'):
                old_alias_map = set(formfield.queryset.query.alias_map)
                formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.complex_filter(limit_choices_to)
                new_alias_map = set(formfield.queryset.query.alias_map)
                added_aliases = new_alias_map - old_alias_map
                has_duplicate_joins = False
                for alias in added_aliases:
                    join = formfield.queryset.query.alias_map[alias]
                    if hasattr(join, 'join_field') and (
                        getattr(join.join_field, 'many_to_many', False) or 
                        getattr(join.join_field, 'one_to_many', False)
                    ):
                        has_duplicate_joins = True
                        break
                if has_duplicate_joins:
                    formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.distinct()
            else:
                formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.complex_filter(limit_choices_to)

form = OrderForm()
# Reset queryset to test our function
form.fields['category'].queryset = Category.objects.all()
apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(form.fields['category'])

print("Choices in form:")
for choice in form.fields['category'].choices:
    print(choice)

EOF

import os

file_path = 'django/forms/models.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):
    \"\"\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\"\"\"
    if hasattr(formfield, 'queryset') and hasattr(formfield, 'get_limit_choices_to'):
        limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()
        if limit_choices_to is not None:
            formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.complex_filter(limit_choices_to)"""

new_code = """def apply_limit_choices_to_to_formfield(formfield):
    \"\"\"Apply limit_choices_to to the formfield's queryset if needed.\"\"\"
    if hasattr(formfield, 'queryset') and hasattr(formfield, 'get_limit_choices_to'):
        limit_choices_to = formfield.get_limit_choices_to()
        if limit_choices_to is not None:
            if hasattr(formfield.queryset, 'query'):
                old_alias_map = set(formfield.queryset.query.alias_map)
                formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.complex_filter(limit_choices_to)
                new_alias_map = set(formfield.queryset.query.alias_map)
                added_aliases = new_alias_map - old_alias_map
                has_duplicate_joins = False
                for alias in added_aliases:
                    join = formfield.queryset.query.alias_map[alias]
                    if hasattr(join, 'join_field') and (
                        getattr(join.join_field, 'many_to_many', False) or 
                        getattr(join.join_field, 'one_to_many', False)
                    ):
                        has_duplicate_joins = True
                        break
                if has_duplicate_joins:
                    formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.distinct()
            else:
                formfield.queryset = formfield.queryset.complex_filter(limit_choices_to)"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find old code.")

with open('./django/contrib/admin/views/autocomplete.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

            'results': [
                self.serialize_result(obj, to_field_name) for obj in context['object_list']
            ],
            'pagination': {'more': context['page_obj'].has_next()},
        })"""

            'results': [

            ],

        })"""

        \"\"\"

        results list of the JSON response.
        \"\"\"
        return {'id': str(getattr(obj, to_field_name)), 'text': str(obj)}

    def get_paginator(self, *args, **kwargs):"""

content = content.replace("    def get_paginator(self, *args, **kwargs):", new_method)

with open('./django/contrib/admin/views/autocomplete.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

    # Create database tables
    from django.core.management import call_command
    call_command('makemigrations', 'testapp', verbosity=0, interactive=False)
    call_command('migrate', verbosity=0, interactive=False)

    # Create test data
    author = Author.objects.create(name='Test Author')
    book = Book.objects.create(title='Test Book', author=author)

    # Test the readonly field URL
    from django.contrib.admin.helpers import AdminForm

            remote_field.model._meta.app_label,
            remote_field.model._meta.model_name,
        )
        url = reverse(url_name, args=[remote_obj.pk], current_app=book_admin.admin_site.name)
        print(f"Current app reverse: {url}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Current app reverse failed: {e}")

    url_html = readonly_field.get_admin_url(remote_field, remote_obj)
    print(f"Generated URL HTML: {url_html}")

    if '/custom-admin/' in url_html:
        print("SUCCESS: Custom admin URL generated correctly.")
    else:
        print("FAILURE: Custom admin URL not generated correctly.")

cat << 'EOF' > patch_compiler.py
import re

with open('django/db/models/sql/compiler.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """
            def local_setter(final_field, obj, from_obj):
                # Set a reverse fk object when relation is non-empty.
                if from_obj:
                    final_field.remote_field.set_cached_value(from_obj, obj)
"""

new_code = """
            def local_setter(final_field, obj, from_obj):
                # Set a reverse fk object when relation is non-empty.
                if from_obj and isinstance(obj, final_field.model):
                    final_field.remote_field.set_cached_value(from_obj, obj)
"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open('django/db/models/sql/compiler.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Old code not found.")
EOF

import datetime

from django.contrib.admin import ModelAdmin
from django.contrib.admin.templatetags.admin_modify import submit_row
from django.contrib.auth.admin import UserAdmin
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.test import RequestFactory, TestCase
from django.urls import reverse

from .admin import ArticleAdmin, site
from .models import Article, Question
from .tests import AdminViewBasicTestCase

class AdminTemplateTagsSaveAsNewTest(AdminViewBasicTestCase):
    request_factory = RequestFactory()

    def test_submit_row_save_as_new(self):
        request = self.request_factory.get(
            reverse("admin:auth_user_change", args=[self.superuser.pk])
        )
        request.user = self.superuser
        admin = UserAdmin(User, site)
        admin.save_as = True
        response = admin.change_view(
            request, str(self.superuser.pk)
        )
        template_context = submit_row(response.context_data)
        self.assertIs(template_context["show_save_as_new"], True)

        # Now test without add permission
        response.context_data["has_add_permission"] = False
        template_context = submit_row(response.context_data)
        self.assertIs(template_context["show_save_as_new"], False)

EOF

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.core.management import call_command
from django.db import models
from django.contrib import admin
from django.core.checks import run_checks

settings.configure(
    INSTALLED_APPS=[
        'django.contrib.admin',
        'django.contrib.contenttypes',
        'django.contrib.auth',
        'django.contrib.messages',
        'django.contrib.sessions',
        'testapp',
    ],
    DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}},
    SECRET_KEY='secret',
    MIDDLEWARE=[],
    ROOT_URLCONF='',
)

os.makedirs('testapp', exist_ok=True)
with open('testapp/__init__.py', 'w') as f:
    pass

with open('testapp/models.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write('''
from django.db import models

class Question(models.Model):
    pass

class Choice(models.Model):
    question = models.ForeignKey(Question, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    questions = models.ManyToManyField(Question, related_name="choices")
    one_question = models.OneToOneField(Question, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name="one_choice")
''')

django.setup()

from testapp.models import Question, Choice

fields = [
    ('Choice.question (ForeignKey)', Choice._meta.get_field('question')),
    ('Choice.questions (ManyToManyField)', Choice._meta.get_field('questions')),
    ('Choice.one_question (OneToOneField)', Choice._meta.get_field('one_question')),
    ('Question.choice (ManyToOneRel)', Question._meta.get_field('choice')),
    ('Question.choices (ManyToManyRel)', Question._meta.get_field('choices')),
    ('Question.one_choice (OneToOneRel)', Question._meta.get_field('one_choice')),
]

for name, field in fields:
    print(f"{name}:")
    print(f"  many_to_many: {getattr(field, 'many_to_many', False)}")
    print(f"  one_to_many: {getattr(field, 'one_to_many', False)}")
    print(f"  many_to_one: {getattr(field, 'many_to_one', False)}")
    print(f"  one_to_one: {getattr(field, 'one_to_one', False)}")
    print()

import re

with open('django/contrib/admin/checks.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """        try:
            field = obj.model._meta.get_field(item)
        except FieldDoesNotExist:
            try:
                field = getattr(obj.model, item)
            except AttributeError:
                return [
                    checks.Error(
                        "The value of '%s' refers to '%s', which is not a "
                        "callable, an attribute of '%s', or an attribute or "
                        "method on '%s'."
                        % (
                            label,
                            item,
                            obj.__class__.__name__,
                            obj.model._meta.label,
                        ),
                        obj=obj.__class__,
                        id="admin.E108",
                    )
                ]
        if isinstance(field, models.ManyToManyField) or (
            getattr(field, "rel", None) and field.rel.field.many_to_one
        ):
            return [
                checks.Error(
                    f"The value of '{label}' must not be a many-to-many field or a "
                    f"reverse foreign key.",
                    obj=obj.__class__,
                    id="admin.E109",
                )
            ]
        return []"""

new_code = """        try:
            field = obj.model._meta.get_field(item)
        except FieldDoesNotExist:
            try:
                field = getattr(obj.model, item)
            except AttributeError:
                return [
                    checks.Error(
                        "The value of '%s' refers to '%s', which is not a "
                        "callable, an attribute of '%s', or an attribute or "
                        "method on '%s'."
                        % (
                            label,
                            item,
                            obj.__class__.__name__,
                            obj.model._meta.label,
                        ),
                        obj=obj.__class__,
                        id="admin.E108",
                    )
                ]
        else:
            if not hasattr(obj.model, item):
                return [
                    checks.Error(
                        "The value of '%s' refers to '%s', which is not a "
                        "callable, an attribute of '%s', or an attribute or "
                        "method on '%s'."
                        % (
                            label,
                            item,
                            obj.__class__.__name__,
                            obj.model._meta.label,
                        ),
                        obj=obj.__class__,
                        id="admin.E108",
                    )
                ]

        is_m2m = getattr(field, "many_to_many", False) or (
            getattr(field, "rel", None) and getattr(field.rel, "many_to_many", False)
        )
        is_reverse_fk = getattr(field, "one_to_many", False) or (
            getattr(field, "rel", None) and getattr(field.rel, "one_to_many", False)
        )
        if is_m2m or is_reverse_fk:
            return [
                checks.Error(
                    f"The value of '{label}' must not be a many-to-many field or a "
                    f"reverse foreign key.",
                    obj=obj.__class__,
                    id="admin.E109",
                )
            ]
        return []"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open('django/contrib/admin/checks.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully!")
else:
    print("Could not find old code!")

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig = plt.figure(dpi=100)

# Mock _set_dpi to update _original_dpi
original_set_dpi = fig._set_dpi
def new_set_dpi(dpi, forward=True):
    original_set_dpi(dpi, forward=forward)
    if hasattr(fig, 'canvas') and hasattr(fig.canvas, '_device_pixel_ratio'):
        fig._original_dpi = dpi / fig.canvas._device_pixel_ratio
    else:
        fig._original_dpi = dpi
fig._set_dpi = new_set_dpi

# Mock _set_device_pixel_ratio to change order
def new_set_device_pixel_ratio(ratio):
    if fig.canvas._device_pixel_ratio == ratio:
        return False
    dpi = ratio * fig._original_dpi
    fig.canvas._device_pixel_ratio = ratio
    fig._set_dpi(dpi, forward=False)
    return True
fig.canvas._set_device_pixel_ratio = new_set_device_pixel_ratio

print("Initial:")
print("dpi:", fig.dpi)
print("original_dpi:", fig._original_dpi)

fig.canvas._set_device_pixel_ratio(2)
print("After ratio=2:")
print("dpi:", fig.dpi)
print("original_dpi:", fig._original_dpi)

fig.dpi = 150
print("After user change to 150:")
print("dpi:", fig.dpi)
print("original_dpi:", fig._original_dpi)

fig.canvas._set_device_pixel_ratio(1)
print("After ratio=1:")
print("dpi:", fig.dpi)
print("original_dpi:", fig._original_dpi)

    original_set_dpi(self, dpi, forward=forward)
    if hasattr(self, 'canvas') and hasattr(self.canvas, '_device_pixel_ratio'):

    if fig.canvas._device_pixel_ratio == ratio:

    dpi = ratio * fig._original_dpi

    fig._set_dpi(dpi, forward=False)

cat << 'EOF' > patch_colors.py
import re

with open('lib/matplotlib/colors.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

new_autoscale_none = """    def autoscale_None(self, A):
        \"\"\"If vmin or vmax are not set, use the min/max of *A* to set them.\"\"\"
        A = np.asanyarray(A)
        changed = False
        with self.callbacks.blocked():
            if self.vmin is None and A.size:
                self.vmin = A.min()
                changed = True
            if self.vmax is None and A.size:
                self.vmax = A.max()
                changed = True
        if changed:
            self._changed()"""

content = re.sub(r'    def autoscale_None\(self, A\):.*?        self\._changed\(\)', new_autoscale_none, content, flags=re.DOTALL)

with open('lib/matplotlib/colors.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

                self.vmax = None
            else:
                self.vmin = self.vcenter - abs(halfrange)

content = re.sub(r'    @halfrange\.setter\n    def halfrange\(self, halfrange\):\n        if halfrange is None:\n            self\.vmin = None\n            self\.vmax = None\n        else:\n            self\.vmin = self\.vcenter - abs\(halfrange\)\n            self\.vmax = self\.vcenter \+ abs\(halfrange\)', new_halfrange, content, flags=re.DOTALL)

with open('lib/matplotlib/colors.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

import re

with open('lib/matplotlib/colors.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_autoscale = """    def autoscale(self, A):
        \"\"\"Set *vmin*, *vmax* to min, max of *A*.\"\"\"
        self.vmin = self.vmax = None
        self.autoscale_None(A)"""

new_autoscale = """    def autoscale(self, A):
        \"\"\"Set *vmin*, *vmax* to min, max of *A*.\"\"\"
        with self.callbacks.blocked():
            # We don't want to call _changed multiple times.
            self.vmin = self.vmax = None
            self.autoscale_None(A)
        self._changed()"""

content = content.replace(old_autoscale, new_autoscale)

old_autoscale_none = """    def autoscale_None(self, A):
        \"\"\"If vmin or vmax are not set, use the min/max of *A* to set them.\"\"\"
        A = np.asanyarray(A)
        if self.vmin is None and A.size:
            self.vmin = A.min()
        if self.vmax is None and A.size:
            self.vmax = A.max()"""

new_autoscale_none = """    def autoscale_None(self, A):
        \"\"\"If vmin or vmax are not set, use the min/max of *A* to set them.\"\"\"
        A = np.asanyarray(A)

        with self.callbacks.blocked():

                changed = True

    f.write(content)

import matplotlib.colorbar as colorbar

def patch_process_values():
    with open('lib/matplotlib/colorbar.py', 'r') as f:
        lines = f.readlines()
    
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if 'if not self.norm.scaled():' in line:
            lines[i+1] = '            try:\n'
            lines[i+2] = '                self.mappable.autoscale_None()\n'
            lines.insert(i+3, '            except ValueError:\n')
            lines.insert(i+4, '                pass\n')
            lines.insert(i+5, '            except TypeError:\n')
            lines.insert(i+6, '                pass\n')
            lines.insert(i+7, '            if not self.norm.scaled():\n')
            lines.insert(i+8, '                self.norm.autoscale_None([0, 1])\n')
            break
            
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if 'b = self.norm.inverse(b)' in line:
            lines[i] = '            try:\n'

import sys

file_path = '/testbed/lib/mpl_toolkits/axes_grid1/axes_grid.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_func = """def _tick_only(ax, bottom_on, left_on):
    bottom_off = not bottom_on
    left_off = not left_on
    ax.axis["bottom"].toggle(ticklabels=bottom_off, label=bottom_off)
    ax.axis["left"].toggle(ticklabels=left_off, label=left_off)"""

new_func = """def _tick_only(ax, bottom_on, left_on):
    bottom_off = not bottom_on
    left_off = not left_on
    
    if isinstance(ax.axis, dict):
        ax.axis["bottom"].toggle(ticklabels=bottom_off, label=bottom_off)
        ax.axis["left"].toggle(ticklabels=left_off, label=left_off)
    else:
        ax.tick_params(labelbottom=bottom_off, labelleft=left_off)
        ax.xaxis.label.set_visible(bottom_off)
        ax.yaxis.label.set_visible(left_off)"""

if old_func in content:
    content = content.replace(old_func, new_func)
    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully")
else:
    print("Could not find the function to patch")

class MockAdapter(BaseAdapter):
    def __init__(self):
        self.reqs = []
        super(MockAdapter, self).__init__()

    def send(self, request, **kwargs):
        self.reqs.append(request)
        resp = Response()
        resp.request = request
        resp.url = request.url

            # First request: POST to /do_something
            resp.status_code = 303
            resp.headers['location'] = '/new_thing_1513'
        elif len(self.reqs) == 2:
            # Second request: GET to /new_thing_1513
            resp.status_code = 307
            resp.headers['location'] = '//failover.example.com/new_thing_1513'
        else:
            # Third request: GET to failover
            resp.status_code = 200
            
        return resp

    def close(self):
        pass

session = Session()
session.mount('http://', MockAdapter())

import ast
from bisect import bisect_right

def get_statement_startend2(lineno: int, node: ast.AST):
    values = []
    for x in ast.walk(node):
        if isinstance(x, (ast.stmt, ast.ExceptHandler)):
            # In Python 3.8+, the lineno of a FunctionDef/ClassDef/AsyncFunctionDef
            # is the line of the def/class keyword, not the first decorator.
            # To avoid including decorators in the preceding statement's range,
            # we use the line of the first decorator if present.
            min_lineno = x.lineno
            if hasattr(x, "decorator_list") and x.decorator_list:
                min_lineno = x.decorator_list[0].lineno
            values.append(min_lineno - 1)
            for name in ("finalbody", "orelse"):
                val = getattr(x, name, None)
                if val:
                    values.append(val[0].lineno - 1 - 1)
    values.sort()
    insert_index = bisect_right(values, lineno)
    start = values[insert_index - 1]
    if insert_index >= len(values):
        end = None
    else:
        end = values[insert_index]
    return start, end

code = """

import ast
from bisect import bisect_right
from typing import List, Optional, Tuple

def get_statement_startend2(lineno: int, node: ast.AST) -> Tuple[int, Optional[int]]:
    # Flatten all statements and except handlers into one lineno-list.
    # AST's line numbers start indexing at 1.
    values: List[int] = []
    for x in ast.walk(node):
        if isinstance(x, (ast.stmt, ast.ExceptHandler)):
            min_lineno = x.lineno
            if hasattr(x, "decorator_list") and x.decorator_list:
                min_lineno = x.decorator_list[0].lineno
            values.append(min_lineno - 1)
            for name in ("finalbody", "orelse"):
                val: Optional[List[ast.stmt]] = getattr(x, name, None)
                if val:
                    # Treat the finally/orelse part as its own statement.
                    val_min_lineno = val[0].lineno
                    if hasattr(val[0], "decorator_list") and val[0].decorator_list:
                        val_min_lineno = val[0].decorator_list[0].lineno
                    values.append(val_min_lineno - 1 - 1)
    values.sort()
    insert_index = bisect_right(values, lineno)
    start = values[insert_index - 1]
    if insert_index >= len(values):
        end = None
    else:
        end = values[insert_index]
    return start, end
EOF

import sys

with open('sklearn/preprocessing/label.py', 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Find transform method
transform_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "def transform(self, y):" in line:
        transform_idx = i
        break

for i in range(transform_idx, len(lines)):
    if "y = column_or_1d(y, warn=True)" in lines[i]:
        lines.insert(i + 1, "        if len(y) == 0:\n            return np.array([], dtype=np.intp)\n")
        break

# Find inverse_transform method
inv_transform_idx = -1
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if "def inverse_transform(self, y):" in line:
        inv_transform_idx = i
        break

for i in range(inv_transform_idx, len(lines)):
    if "y = np.asarray(y)" in lines[i]:
        lines.insert(i + 1, "        if len(y) == 0:\n            return np.array([], dtype=self.classes_.dtype)\n")
        break

with open('sklearn/preprocessing/label.py', 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(lines)
EOF

def test_label_encoder_empty_array():
    # Test that LabelEncoder.transform and inverse_transform work with empty arrays
    le = LabelEncoder()
    le.fit(['a', 'b', 'c'])
    
    # transform empty list
    assert_array_equal(le.transform([]), np.array([], dtype=np.intp))
    
    # transform empty array
    assert_array_equal(le.transform(np.array([])), np.array([], dtype=np.intp))
    
    # inverse_transform empty list
    assert_array_equal(le.inverse_transform([]), np.array([], dtype=le.classes_.dtype))
    
    # inverse_transform empty array
    assert_array_equal(le.inverse_transform(np.array([])), np.array([], dtype=le.classes_.dtype))

import re

with open('django/forms/formsets.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

replacement = """class ManagementForm(Form):
    \"\"\"
    Keep track of how many form instances are displayed on the page. If adding
    new forms via JavaScript, you should increment the count field of this form
    as well.
    \"\"\"

    TOTAL_FORMS = IntegerField(widget=HiddenInput)
    INITIAL_FORMS = IntegerField(widget=HiddenInput)
    # MIN_NUM_FORM_COUNT and MAX_NUM_FORM_COUNT are output with the rest of the
    # management form, but only for the convenience of client-side code. The
    # POST value of them returned from the client is not checked.
    MIN_NUM_FORMS = IntegerField(required=False, widget=HiddenInput)
    MAX_NUM_FORMS = IntegerField(required=False, widget=HiddenInput)

    @property
    def template_name(self):
        return (
            "django/forms/div.html"
            if self.renderer.form_template_name == "django/forms/default.html"
            else self.renderer.form_template_name
        )"""

content = re.sub(r'class ManagementForm\(Form\):.*?MAX_NUM_FORMS = IntegerField\(required=False, widget=HiddenInput\)', replacement, content, flags=re.DOTALL)

with open('django/forms/formsets.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > test_decompose.py
import enum

def decompose(flag):
    if hasattr(enum, '_decompose'):
        members, uncovered = enum._decompose(flag.__class__, flag.value)
        real_members = [m for m in members if m.name is not None]
        uncovered = flag.value
        for m in real_members:
            uncovered &= ~m.value
        return real_members, uncovered
    else:
        members = list(flag)
        uncovered = flag.value
        for m in members:
            uncovered &= ~m.value
        return members, uncovered

class MyFlag(enum.IntFlag):
    A = 1
    B = 2

flags = MyFlag.A | MyFlag.B | 4
print(decompose(flags))
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > patch_forms.py
with open('django/contrib/auth/forms.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_str = 'password.help_text = password.help_text.format(f"../../{self.instance.pk}/password/")'
new_str = 'password.help_text = password.help_text.format(\n                f"../../{self.instance.pk}/password/" if self.instance.pk else "../password/"\n            )'

content = content.replace(old_str, new_str)

with open('django/contrib/auth/forms.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

from django.core.cache.backends.filebased import FileBasedCache

cache = FileBasedCache('/tmp/django_cache_test', {'max_entries': 300})

def worker():
    for i in range(100):
        cache.set('key', 'value', timeout=0.0001)
        cache.has_key('key')

threads = []
for i in range(10):
    t = threading.Thread(target=worker)
    threads.append(t)
    t.start()

for t in threads:
    t.join()

print("Done without errors")

cat << 'EOF' > patch.py
import os

file_path = 'django/core/cache/backends/filebased.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """    def has_key(self, key, version=None):
        fname = self._key_to_file(key, version)
        if os.path.exists(fname):
            with open(fname, "rb") as f:
                return not self._is_expired(f)
        return False"""

new_code = """    def has_key(self, key, version=None):
        fname = self._key_to_file(key, version)
        if os.path.exists(fname):
            try:
                with open(fname, "rb") as f:
                    return not self._is_expired(f)
            except FileNotFoundError:
                return False
        return False"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully")
else:
    print("Could not find old code")
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > patch_only.py
import re

with open('django/db/models/sql/query.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """    def _get_only_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
        if select_mask is None:
            select_mask = {}
        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
        # Only include fields mentioned in the mask.
        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
            field = opts.get_field(field_name)
            field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
            if field_mask:
                if not field.is_relation:
                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
                related_model = field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model
                self._get_only_select_mask(
                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
                )
        return select_mask"""

new_code = """    def _get_only_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
        if select_mask is None:
            select_mask = {}
        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
        # Only include fields mentioned in the mask.
        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
            field = opts.get_field(field_name)
            if hasattr(field, "field"):
                field = field.field
                related_model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
            else:
                related_model = field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model if field.is_relation else None
            field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
            if field_mask:
                if not field.is_relation:
                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
                self._get_only_select_mask(
                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
                )
        return select_mask"""

content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)

with open('django/db/models/sql/query.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

cat << 'EOF' > patch_only3.py
import re

with open('django/db/models/sql/query.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """    def _get_only_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
        if select_mask is None:
            select_mask = {}
        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
        # Only include fields mentioned in the mask.
        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
            field = opts.get_field(field_name)
            if hasattr(field, "field"):
                field = field.field
                related_model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
            else:
                related_model = field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model if field.is_relation else None
            field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
            if field_mask:
                if not field.is_relation:
                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
                self._get_only_select_mask(
                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
                )
        return select_mask"""

new_code = """    def _get_only_select_mask(self, opts, mask, select_mask=None):
        if select_mask is None:
            select_mask = {}
        select_mask[opts.pk] = {}
        # Only include fields mentioned in the mask.
        for field_name, field_mask in mask.items():
            field = opts.get_field(field_name)
            is_reverse = hasattr(field, "field")
            if is_reverse:
                field = field.field
            field_select_mask = select_mask.setdefault(field, {})
            if field_mask:
                if not field.is_relation:
                    raise FieldError(next(iter(field_mask)))
                if is_reverse:
                    related_model = field.model._meta.concrete_model
                else:
                    related_model = field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model
                self._get_only_select_mask(
                    related_model._meta, field_mask, field_select_mask
                )
        return select_mask"""

content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)

with open('django/db/models/sql/query.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

import os
import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import models

settings.configure(
    DATABASES={
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
            'NAME': ':memory:',
        }
    },
    INSTALLED_APPS=['__main__'],
)

django.setup()

class MyModel(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)
    value = models.IntegerField()

    class Meta:
        app_label = '__main__'

django.core.management.call_command('migrate', verbosity=0, interactive=False)

# Create table manually since it's not in a real app
with django.db.connection.cursor() as cursor:
    cursor.execute('''
        CREATE TABLE main_mymodel (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
            value INTEGER NOT NULL
        )
    ''')

# Insert initial data
MyModel.objects.create(name='test1', value=1)

# Bulk create with update_conflicts
objs = [
    MyModel(name='test1', value=2),
    MyModel(name='test2', value=2),
]

MyModel.objects.bulk_create(
    objs,
    update_conflicts=True,
    update_fields=['value'],
    unique_fields=['name']
)

print("IDs after bulk_create:")
for obj in objs:
    print(f"{obj.name}: {obj.id}")

cat << 'EOF' > test_version_info.py
from packaging.version import parse as parse_version

def _parse_to_version_info(version_str):
    v = parse_version(version_str)
    if v.release is None:
        return ()
    major = v.release[0]
    minor = v.release[1] if len(v.release) > 1 else 0
    micro = v.release[2] if len(v.release) > 2 else 0
    if v.pre is not None:
        releaselevel = v.pre[0]
        serial = v.pre[1]
    elif v.dev is not None:
        releaselevel = 'dev'
        serial = v.dev
    elif v.post is not None:
        releaselevel = 'post'
        serial = v.post
    else:
        releaselevel = 'final'
        serial = 0
    return (major, minor, micro, releaselevel, serial)

print(_parse_to_version_info('3.4.2'))
print(_parse_to_version_info('3.4.2rc1'))
print(_parse_to_version_info('3.4.2.post1324+gb7d059198'))
print(_parse_to_version_info('3.4.2.dev1'))
EOF

    See:

    https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.version_info
    \"\"\"
    v = parse_version(version_str)

        return _VersionInfo(v.major, v.minor, v.micro, 'final', 0)
    elif v.dev is not None:
        return _VersionInfo(v.major, v.minor, v.micro, 'alpha', v.dev)

def __getattr__(name):
    if name in ("__version__", "__version_info__"):

        __version__ = _get_version()
        global __version_info__  # cache it.
        __version_info__ = _parse_to_version_info(__version__)
        return __version__ if name == "__version__" else __version_info__
    raise AttributeError(f"module {__name__!r} has no attribute {name!r}")

        root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
        if (root / ".git").exists() and not (root / ".git/shallow").exists():
            __version__ = setuptools_scm.get_version(
                root=root,
                version_scheme="post-release",
                local_scheme="node-and-date",
                fallback_version=_version.version,
            )
        else:  # Get the version from the _version.py setuptools_scm file.
            __version__ = _version.version
        return __version__
    raise AttributeError(f"module {__name__!r} has no attribute {name!r}")"""

    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    norm = mpl.colors.BoundaryNorm(np.linspace(-4, 4, 5), 256)

    norm = mpl.colors.NoNorm()
    X = np.random.randint(0, 256, (10, 10))
    pc = ax.imshow(X, cmap='RdBu_r', norm=norm)
    
    # Should not crash
    res = pc.format_cursor_data(X[0, 0])
    assert res is not None

import sys

file_path = 'lib/mpl_toolkits/mplot3d/art3d.py'
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Remove duplicate lines
new_lines = []
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if line == '        if not self.get_visible():\n' and lines[i+1] == '            return\n' and lines[i+2] == '        if not self.get_visible():\n':
        continue
    if line == '            return\n' and lines[i-1] == '        if not self.get_visible():\n' and lines[i+1] == '        if not self.get_visible():\n':
        continue
    new_lines.append(line)

    f.writelines(new_lines)

import re

with open('lib/mpl_toolkits/mplot3d/art3d.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

def make_atomic(func_name, orig_body, new_body):
    global content
    content = content.replace(orig_body, new_body)

# text_2d_to_3d
text_orig = """def text_2d_to_3d(obj, z=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a Text to a Text3D object.\"\"\"
    obj.__class__ = Text3D
    obj.set_3d_properties(z, zdir)"""

text_new = """def text_2d_to_3d(obj, z=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a Text to a Text3D object.\"\"\"
    orig_class = obj.__class__
    obj.__class__ = Text3D
    try:
        obj.set_3d_properties(z, zdir)
    except Exception:
        obj.__class__ = orig_class
        raise"""

make_atomic('text_2d_to_3d', text_orig, text_new)

# line_2d_to_3d
line_orig = """def line_2d_to_3d(line, zs=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a 2D line to 3D.\"\"\"

    line.__class__ = Line3D
    line.set_3d_properties(zs, zdir)"""

line_new = """def line_2d_to_3d(line, zs=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a 2D line to 3D.\"\"\"

    orig_class = line.__class__
    line.__class__ = Line3D
    try:
        line.set_3d_properties(zs, zdir)
    except Exception:
        line.__class__ = orig_class
        raise"""

make_atomic('line_2d_to_3d', line_orig, line_new)

# line_collection_2d_to_3d
lc_orig = """def line_collection_2d_to_3d(col, zs=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a LineCollection to a Line3DCollection object.\"\"\"
    segments3d = _paths_to_3d_segments(col.get_paths(), zs, zdir)
    col.__class__ = Line3DCollection
    col.set_segments(segments3d)"""

lc_new = """def line_collection_2d_to_3d(col, zs=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a LineCollection to a Line3DCollection object.\"\"\"
    segments3d = _paths_to_3d_segments(col.get_paths(), zs, zdir)
    orig_class = col.__class__
    col.__class__ = Line3DCollection
    try:
        col.set_segments(segments3d)
    except Exception:
        col.__class__ = orig_class
        raise"""

make_atomic('line_collection_2d_to_3d', lc_orig, lc_new)

# patch_2d_to_3d
patch_orig = """def patch_2d_to_3d(patch, z=0, zdir='z'):
    \"\"\"Convert a Patch to a Patch3D object.\"\"\"
    verts = _get_patch_verts(patch)
    patch.__class__ = Patch3D

cat << 'EOF' > patch.py
import re

with open('lib/matplotlib/style/core.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Extract the list of deprecated seaborn styles
seaborn_styles_match = re.search(r'if s in \[\n(.*?)\]:', content, re.DOTALL)
seaborn_styles_str = seaborn_styles_match.group(1)

# Create the _DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES constant
deprecated_styles_code = f"""
_DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES = [
{seaborn_styles_str}]
"""

# Replace the list in fix_style with the constant
content = content.replace(f"if s in [\n{seaborn_styles_str}]:", "if s in _DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES:")

# Add the _StyleLibrary class
style_library_code = """
class _StyleLibrary(dict):
    def __getitem__(self, key):
        if key in _DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES:
            _api.warn_deprecated(
                "3.6", message="The seaborn styles shipped by Matplotlib "
                "are deprecated since %(since)s, as they no longer "
                "correspond to the styles shipped by seaborn. However, "
                "they will remain available as 'seaborn-v0_8-<style>'. "
                "Alternatively, directly use the seaborn API instead.")
            key = key.replace("seaborn", "seaborn-v0_8")
        return super().__getitem__(key)

    def get(self, key, default=None):
        if key in _DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES:
            _api.warn_deprecated(
                "3.6", message="The seaborn styles shipped by Matplotlib "
                "are deprecated since %(since)s, as they no longer "
                "correspond to the styles shipped by seaborn. However, "
                "they will remain available as 'seaborn-v0_8-<style>'. "
                "Alternatively, directly use the seaborn API instead.")
            key = key.replace("seaborn", "seaborn-v0_8")
        return super().get(key, default)

    def __contains__(self, key):
        if key in _DEPRECATED_SEABORN_STYLES:
            return True
        return super().__contains__(key)
"""

# Insert the constant and class before _base_library = read_style_directory(BASE_LIBRARY_PATH)
content = content.replace(
    "# Load style library\n# ==================",
    deprecated_styles_code + style_library_code + "\n# Load style library\n# =================="
)

# Change library = None to library = None (wait, reload_library sets it)
# In reload_library:
# library = update_user_library(_base_library)
# We need to make sure library is a _StyleLibrary.
# update_user_library returns a dict.
# Let's change reload_library:
reload_library_code = """def reload_library():
    \"\"\"Reload the style library.\"\"\"
    global library
    library = _StyleLibrary()
    library.update(update_user_library(_base_library))
    available[:] = sorted(library.keys())"""

content = re.sub(r'def reload_library\(\):.*?available\[:\] = sorted\(library\.keys\(\)\)', reload_library_code, content, flags=re.DOTALL)

with open('lib/matplotlib/style/core.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
EOF

import sys

with open('django/core/checks/model_checks.py', 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

new_lines = []
skip = False
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if line.startswith('from django.core.checks import Error, Tags, register'):
        new_lines.append('from django.conf import settings\n')
        new_lines.append('from django.core.checks import Error, Tags, Warning, register\n')
    elif "for db_table, model_labels in db_table_models.items():" in line:
        new_lines.append("    if settings.DATABASE_ROUTERS:\n")
        new_lines.append("        error_class, error_id = Warning, 'models.W035'\n")
        new_lines.append("        error_hint = (\n")
        new_lines.append("            'You have configured settings.DATABASE_ROUTERS. Verify that %s '\n")
        new_lines.append("            'are correctly routed to separate databases.'\n")
        new_lines.append("        )\n")
        new_lines.append("    else:\n")
        new_lines.append("        error_class, error_id = Error, 'models.E028'\n")
        new_lines.append("        error_hint = None\n")
        new_lines.append(line)
        skip = True
    elif skip and "for index_name, model_labels in indexes.items():" in line:
        skip = False
        new_lines.append(line)
    elif skip:

import psycopg2

conn = psycopg2.connect(dbname='postgres', user='postgres', password='', host='localhost', port='5432')
cur = conn.cursor()

cur.execute('''
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_a (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY);
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_ab (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, a_id INT, b_id INT, status INT);
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_c (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, a_id INT, status INT);

INSERT INTO test_a VALUES (1), (2) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
INSERT INTO test_ab (a_id, b_id, status) VALUES (1, 1, 10), (2, 1, 10) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
''')

try:
    cur.execute('''
    SELECT 
        (SELECT U0.status FROM test_ab U0 WHERE U0.a_id = test_a.id AND U0.b_id = 1) AS status, 
        COUNT((SELECT U0.status FROM test_ab U0 WHERE U0.a_id = test_a.id AND U0.b_id = 1)) AS total_count 
    FROM test_a 
    GROUP BY (SELECT U0.status FROM test_ab U0 WHERE U0.a_id = test_a.id AND U0.b_id = 1)
    ''')
    print(cur.fetchall())
except Exception as e:
    print("Error:", e)

conn.rollback()

from django.db.migrations.state import ProjectState

from django.db.backends.sqlite3.schema import DatabaseSchemaEditor

        app_label = '__main__'

    class Meta:

import django
from django.conf import settings
from django.db import models, migrations
from django.db.migrations.state import ProjectState, ModelState
from django.db import connection

settings.configure(
    INSTALLED_APPS=['__main__'],
    DATABASES={'default': {'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3', 'NAME': ':memory:'}}
)
django.setup()

state1 = ProjectState()
state1.add_model(ModelState('__main__', 'A', [], options={'db_table': 'custom_a'}))
state1.add_model(ModelState('__main__', 'B', [('id', models.AutoField(primary_key=True)), ('a', models.ForeignKey('__main__.A', models.CASCADE))]))

state2 = ProjectState()
state2.add_model(ModelState('__main__', 'C', [], options={'db_table': 'custom_a'}))
state2.add_model(ModelState('__main__', 'B', [('id', models.AutoField(primary_key=True)), ('a', models.ForeignKey('__main__.C', models.CASCADE))]))

op = migrations.RenameModel('A', 'C')
with connection.schema_editor() as schema_editor:
    # We need to create the tables first
    schema_editor.create_model(state1.apps.get_model('__main__', 'A'))
    schema_editor.create_model(state1.apps.get_model('__main__', 'B'))
    
    print("--- Running RenameModel ---")
    op.database_forwards('__main__', schema_editor, state1, state2)
    print("--- Done ---")

cat << 'EOF' > patch.py
import sys

with open('django/utils/functional.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find where to insert __add__ and __radd__
# Let's insert them after __contains__
insert_pos = content.find('    __contains__ = new_method_proxy(operator.contains)')
if insert_pos == -1:
    print("Could not find __contains__")
    sys.exit(1)

insert_pos += len('    __contains__ = new_method_proxy(operator.contains)')

new_methods = """

    # Math operations
    __add__ = new_method_proxy(operator.add)

    def __radd__(self, other):
        if self._wrapped is empty:
            self._setup()
        return other + self._wrapped"""

new_content = content[:insert_pos] + new_methods + content[insert_pos:]

with open('django/utils/functional.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(new_content)

print("Patched django/utils/functional.py")
EOF

with open('requests/adapters.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Add TimeoutError and DecodeError to imports
content = content.replace(
    'from .packages.urllib3.exceptions import ResponseError',
    'from .packages.urllib3.exceptions import ResponseError\nfrom .packages.urllib3.exceptions import TimeoutError as _TimeoutError\nfrom .packages.urllib3.exceptions import DecodeError as _DecodeError'
)

# Add Timeout and ContentDecodingError to exceptions imports
content = content.replace(
    'from .exceptions import (ConnectionError, ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout, SSLError,\n                         ProxyError, RetryError)',
    'from .exceptions import (ConnectionError, ConnectTimeout, ReadTimeout, SSLError,\n                         ProxyError, RetryError, Timeout, ContentDecodingError)'
)

# Add handling for ConnectTimeoutError, _TimeoutError, _DecodeError
old_except = '''        except (_SSLError, _HTTPError) as e:
            if isinstance(e, _SSLError):
                raise SSLError(e, request=request)
            elif isinstance(e, ReadTimeoutError):
                raise ReadTimeout(e, request=request)
            else:
                raise'''

new_except = '''        except (_SSLError, _HTTPError) as e:
            if isinstance(e, _SSLError):
                raise SSLError(e, request=request)
            elif isinstance(e, ReadTimeoutError):
                raise ReadTimeout(e, request=request)
            elif isinstance(e, ConnectTimeoutError):
                raise ConnectTimeout(e, request=request)
            elif isinstance(e, _TimeoutError):
                raise Timeout(e, request=request)
            elif isinstance(e, _DecodeError):
                raise ContentDecodingError(e, request=request)
            else:
                raise'''

content = content.replace(old_except, new_except)

with open('requests/adapters.py', 'w') as f:

# Add __getstate__ and __setstate__ to DraggableBase
# We need to find the end of DraggableBase class or just add it before DraggableOffsetBox

getstate_code = """
    def __getstate__(self):
        state = self.__dict__.copy()
        # The canvas cannot be pickled. It will be restored when the figure is unpickled.
        state.pop('canvas', None)

    def __setstate__(self, state):
        self.__dict__ = state

"""

cat << 'EOF' > patch.py
import re

with open('sklearn/utils/validation.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

old_code = """    dtype_orig = getattr(array, "dtype", None)
    if not hasattr(dtype_orig, 'kind'):
        # not a data type (e.g. a column named dtype in a pandas DataFrame)
        dtype_orig = None"""

new_code = """    dtype_orig = getattr(array, "dtype", None)
    if not hasattr(dtype_orig, 'kind'):
        # not a data type (e.g. a column named dtype in a pandas DataFrame)
        dtype_orig = None

    # check if the object contains several dtypes (typically a pandas
    # DataFrame), and store them. If there is a single dtype, just use it
    if hasattr(array, "dtypes") and hasattr(array.dtypes, '__array__'):
        dtypes = set(array.dtypes)
        if len(dtypes) == 1:
            dtype_orig = dtypes.pop()
        else:
            dtype_orig = np.dtype('object')"""

if old_code in content:
    content = content.replace(old_code, new_code)
    with open('sklearn/utils/validation.py', 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("Patched successfully.")
else:
    print("Could not find the code to patch.")
EOF
python patch.py

import re

with open('sphinx/environment/adapters/indexentries.py', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Replace keyfunc
old_keyfunc = """        # sort the index entries; put all symbols at the front, even those
        # following the letters in ASCII, this is where the chr(127) comes from
        def keyfunc(entry: Tuple[str, List]) -> Tuple[str, str]:
            key, (void, void, category_key) = entry
            if category_key:
                # using specified category key to sort
                key = category_key
            lckey = unicodedata.normalize('NFD', key.lower())
            if lckey.startswith('\\N{RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK}'):
                lckey = lckey[1:]
            if lckey[0:1].isalpha() or lckey.startswith('_'):
                lckey = chr(127) + lckey
            # ensure a determinstic order *within* letters by also sorting on
            # the entry itself
            return (lckey, entry[0])"""

new_keyfunc = """        # sort the index entries; put all symbols at the front, even those
        # following the letters in ASCII, this is where the chr(127) comes from
        def keyfunc(entry: Tuple[str, List]) -> Tuple[Tuple[int, str], str]:
            key, (void, void, category_key) = entry
            if category_key:
                # using specified category key to sort
                key = category_key
            lckey = unicodedata.normalize('NFD', key.lower())
            if lckey.startswith('\\N{RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK}'):
                lckey = lckey[1:]
            
            if lckey[0:1].isalpha() or lckey.startswith('_'):
                flag = 1
            else:
                flag = 0
            
            # ensure a determinstic order *within* letters by also sorting on
            # the entry itself
            return ((flag, lckey), entry[0])"""

content = content.replace(old_keyfunc, new_keyfunc)

# Replace keyfunc2
old_keyfunc2 = """        # sort the sub-index entries
        def keyfunc2(entry: Tuple[str, List]) -> str:
            key = unicodedata.normalize('NFD', entry[0].lower())
            if key.startswith('\\N{RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK}'):
                key = key[1:]
            if key[0:1].isalpha() or key.startswith('_'):
                key = chr(127) + key
            return key"""

new_keyfunc2 = """        # sort the sub-index entries
        def keyfunc2(entry: Tuple[str, List]) -> Tuple[int, str]:
            key = unicodedata.normalize('NFD', entry[0].lower())
            if key.startswith('\\N{RIGHT-TO-LEFT MARK}'):
                key = key[1:]
            
            if key[0:1].isalpha() or key.startswith('_'):
                flag = 1
            else:
                flag = 0
            
            return (flag, key)"""

content = content.replace(old_keyfunc2, new_keyfunc2)

with open('sphinx/environment/adapters/indexentries.py', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)
